verified_against_binary: CLI 2.1.138 + Claude.app 1.6259.1 (cross-checked against CLI 2.1.119 + Claude.app 1.5354.0; v2.11.17 correction pass cross-checked against CLI 2.1.159)
note: v2.11.0 adds Chapter 20 (references/17-verified-new-v2.1.120.md) covering v2.1.119-v2.1.120 — the Cowork-runtime release. L89 (v2.1.119): Claude Cowork's runtime infrastructure goes live. Cowork is the product label for sessions running with CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_KIND='bg' (no 'cowork' string in the bundle — detection is via the BG family). New /background (alias /bg) forks the current main session into the background as a kind:'fork' subagent (reuses L87 fork-subagent infrastructure unchanged); /stop dual-registered (interactive Ink modal + non-interactive headless), only enabled when SESSION_KIND==='bg'; /daemon Ink TUI manages three service categories (assistant, scheduled, remoteControl — the 'remote-control server' is the Cowork Desktop bridge channel); /autocompact re-introduced (token-count parameterized via argumentHint '[auto|<tokens>]', default ~100k, max ~1M, state field autoCompactWindow); Fleet view = standalone 'claude agents' CLI subcommand (NOT a panel) mounting its own Ink TUI dashboard, tracks per-agent PR state (state, title, review, mergeable, mergeStateStatus, checks.passed/failed/pending, additions, deletions), gated on isAgentsFleetEnabled(), batched-fetch toggle tengu_fleetview_pr_batch. Session identity quartet (CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_KIND/ID/NAME/LOG) plus 5-var BG-context check (T1H, vK, uC_) — all 5 vars stripped from env before subprocess spawn so daemon plumbing doesn't leak. Worktree-isolation runtime prompt mutation (bA3) when SESSION_KIND==='bg' && CLAUDE_BG_ISOLATION==='worktree' — system prompt rewritten to force EnterWorktree as first action. PTY recording via internal --bg-pty-host argv mode to CLAUDE_PTY_RECORD file path. CLAUDE_BRIDGE_REATTACH_SESSION/SEQ tokens (L87) consumed exactly once for reattach. Classifier-summary system (the Cowork Desktop status pipeline): surface map (bg/watched/ccr/bridge/desktop/cli) → capabilities (state/summary) → engine (heuristic/llm), three independent kill switches (tengu_classifier_disabled_surfaces, tengu_classifier_summary_kill, tengu_cobalt_wren LLM→heuristic cost circuit-breaker), output schema {status_category, status_detail, needs_action} pushed via notifyMetadataChanged({post_turn_summary}). Pro-trial conversion screens (4 telemetry events) — Cowork is paid-only. 16 new env vars, 62 new tengu_* identifiers (36 background+daemon, 4 fleet, 4 pro-trial, 4 classifier, 5 codename flags, 9 other), /exit description acknowledges bg detach/stop. L90 (v2.1.120): refinement release with one major reveal — persistent daemon install is kill-switched. xQH() aborts with 'daemon service is not installed (service install is disabled in this version; the daemon runs on demand)'. Only on-demand modes available via new CLAUDE_CODE_DAEMON_COLD_START env var: 'transient' (default, silent on-demand) or 'ask' (prompted). Daemon hot-upgrades itself (tengu_daemon_self_restart_on_upgrade). CLAUDE_CODE_LEAN_PROMPT introduces granular per-section prompt-shaping toggle (distinct from L86 wholesale CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE swap) — two leanable sections in v2.1.120 (Bash/ripgrep description gated by tengu_vellum_lantern + Opus-4.7-only; memory-types section gated by tengu_ochre_finch). CLAUDE_EFFORT is NOT an env var (the diff regex was misreading a binary string-table dump): it's a skill/command frontmatter field 'effort:' AND a template-substitution token ${CLAUDE_EFFORT} with values low|medium|high(default)|xhigh resolving to literal English phrases injected by _I(model, effort). CLAUDE_COWORK_MEMORY_GUIDELINES = Cowork's memory-bypass escape hatch — when set, completely replaces the entire memory-injection pipeline (sibling pre-existing _EXTRA_GUIDELINES is the additive form). tengu_memory_write_survey_event = Approve/Reject confirmation dialog for memory file writes with per-write Sonnet-4.6 LLM-generated summary (≤120 chars) and 5-second countdown. CLAUDE_CODE_VERIFY_PROMPT is debugging-workflow discipline (NOT safety) — injects a 3-step reproduce→fix→re-observe instruction; identifies tengu_sparrow_ledger as its dark-launch flag. tengu_plan_mode_violated is observability-only tripwire (no enforcement, no early return) — fires when a non-read-only tool slips past plan-mode permission layer. tengu_bg_retired = idle worker reaper with 6 do-not-retire guards (no-state, not-settled, inflight, session-cron, routine, grace), NOT feature sunset. CLAUDE_AGENTS_AUTO_RELAUNCHED_AT (env key Ih8) plus AUTO_RELAUNCH_UNFOCUSED_MS (oz6 = 1h) and AUTO_RELAUNCH_MIN_INTERVAL_MS (sYK = 6h) rate-limit gates. CLAUDE_CODE_HIDE_CWD blanks cwd in status header for screensharing. CLAUDE_AGENTS_SELECT pre-selects an agent ID across left-arrow→child boundary. /schedule's description simplified (template-literal conditional → static string), no new registration. _FORK_SUBAGENTM 'env var' was a diff-tool string-table-adjacency artifact — NEVER a real var, fixed by rewriting extract_envvars in scripts/diff-versions.sh to use JS-context anchors (process.env.X / 'X' / {X:...}) instead of \b word boundaries. Bundle now embeds VERSION/BUILD_TIME/GIT_SHA literally. Cross-references the Cowork-runtime stack: L37 (Bridge/Remote Control transport that persists transcripts) → L43 (KAIROS daemon characterization) → L77/L85 (sunset of cloud-thin-client model + first CLAUDE_BG_BACKEND public surface) → L86 (OIDC Federation auth, dual-registration pattern) → L87 (/fork + reattach plumbing) → L88 (dual-registration adopters) → L89/L90 (this chapter). v2.10.0 baseline: added Chapter 19 covering v2.1.117-v2.1.118 changes. v2.11.17: correction pass (part a) + diff-tool fix (part b) ahead of the v2.1.159 chapter, cross-checked against CLI 2.1.159. Content baseline stays v2.1.120 (no new lessons yet). Corrections: (1) hook event-type count — the live master hook array is 30 (adds MessageDisplay as the 30th, plus the post-v2.1.90 events the old extractor undercounted); fixed 'all 27 event types' → 30 in SKILL.md description; ch14 (v2.1.113) 'Hook event types: 19' was a diff-tool undercount artifact (contradicted ch9/L10's verified 27) — corrected to 'no additions in v2.1.113' with an explanatory note; ch9/L10's '27' is left intact (point-in-time-correct for v2.1.90). (2) Streaming Tool Execution (ch1) is now UNCONDITIONAL GA in v2.1.159 — the config.gates.streamingToolExecution gate (tengu_streaming_tool_execution2) was deleted, executor built directly (jM_→U__), no escape hatch; added an update note. (3) Lean prompt (ch17/L90) is now DEFAULT in v2.1.159 (CHANGELOG 2.1.154); tengu_vellum_lantern (Bash/ripgrep gate, Opus-4.7-only) is REMOVED from the 2.1.159 bundle (tengu_ochre_finch survives) — added an update note marking the per-section flag table as a v2.1.120 snapshot. (4) ${CLAUDE_EFFORT} substituter minified name is bk(model,effort) in 2.1.159 (was _I in 2.1.120 — identifier drift, mechanism unchanged); effort tiers gained 'max'; Opus 4.8 defaults to 'high'. Part (b): scripts/diff-versions.sh hardened against four artifact classes that caused wrong diffs — (i) extract_hook_types dropped its suffix allowlist and now captures every PascalCase string in the master array (29→30 now tracked, MessageDisplay surfaced); (ii) extract_commands now matches backtick (template-literal) and dynamic (get description(){return COND?...}) descriptions, fixing FALSE REMOVALS (/remote-control was falsely reported removed; /dream is a GENUINE removal — reclassified from a name:'dream' command to a built-in routine alongside /catch-up + /morning-checkin with cron '0 */2 * * *'); also surfaced /exec; (iii) added env-read advisory flagging added env vars with no process.env.X read (markdown/export/allowlist-only/dead, e.g. CLAUDE_CODE_SKILL_NAME/DESCRIPTION, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_GATEWAY); (iv) runtime NOTE warns that routine-table commands show as 'removed'. Full v2.1.138→2.1.159 analysis lives in docs/internal/{diff,deep-dive,update-plan}-2.1.159.md for the forthcoming Chapter 21 (L91+). v2.11.16: documents Cowork plugin-hook exclusion via --setting-sources=user. The desktop spawns the in-VM CLI with `--setting-sources=user` which restricts settings resolution to user scope; plugin-scoped hooks (declared in plugin hooks/hooks.json files) are silently excluded from discovery, while plugin skills/slash-commands/MCP-servers still load via per-plugin --plugin-dir args. Empirically confirmed: 8MB cowork_vm_node.log + 2MB coworkd.log show zero hook references for any Cowork session (named pattern <adj>-<adj>-<word>); same desktop process logs Stop hooks for CCD sessions (pattern local_<uuid>) on the same host. Cross-references upstream issues #16288 (general CLI race in loadPluginHooks dispatch) and #27398 (Cowork-specific scope exclusion, closed as dup). Workaround documented: move hooks to ~/.claude/settings.json (user scope). New L89 subsection 'Plugin hooks dont fire in Cowork sessions' added between 'What the CLI async sub-agent filter actually does' and 'Original v2.11.3 trace'. New Risks Worth Flagging item 9. Cross-references the userconfig-probe plugin from earlier in conversation as a concrete example of the failure mode. Companion gist (303b6213b7a33167b3f98b076a5f81ad) updated with a parallel subsection in the Cowork-mode area, framed generically (no project-specific references). v2.11.15: third correction pass on L89, replacing incrementally-corrected framing with a clean lead. The v2.11.3→v2.11.13→v2.11.14 chain accreted layered corrections that left the section harder to read than the underlying facts warranted. v2.11.15 leads with the simple story (Cowork has no built-in Bash at any dispatch level; shell happens via mcp__workspace__bash deferred MCP tool loaded via ToolSearch) and explains derivatively: why SKILL.md works without allowed-tools (main thread has ToolSearch immediate + mcp__workspace__bash deferred and the model figures out the dispatch); why narrow sub-agent tools-list declarations fail (literal Bash name doesn't resolve, Task canonicalizes to Agent which is in the sub-agent drop set, mcp__workspace__bash isn't declared); and links the actual founder-skills v0.3.0 incident to the right mechanism (declaration named no-op tool names, didn't include mcp__workspace__bash + ToolSearch, resolved to {Read, Glob, Grep}). The fix the team chose (Write/Edit) was correct for their use case but the documented rationale (Cowork strips Bash from sub-agents) was wrong. Lesson now explicitly notes: the team's v0.4.1 architecture (SKILL.md in main thread + analytical sub-agents using Read/Edit/Glob/Grep) is right architecture but the cowork-architecture-and-v0.4.x-learning.md doc rationale needs updating to match. Operational contract subsection unchanged from v2.11.14 (still describes the five constraints accurately). Companion gist (303b6213b7a33167b3f98b076a5f81ad) re-edited with the same clean-lead framing — top-of-gist correction callout simplified; the 'Sub-agent tool-grant filtering' section reorganized to lead with the simple story. v2.11.14: scope-corrects v2.11.13's L89 update. v2.11.13 introduced the right mechanism (host-loop substitution `jie`/`zvt`/`PTi` in the desktop bundle is the gate, not the CLI's `Tw8`/`Jl_` async filter) but framed it as sub-agent-specific. Empirical re-probe in actual Cowork main session (not sub-agent) shows the top-level Cowork session ALSO lacks built-in Bash — only `mcp__workspace__bash` is available, in the deferred tier. The asymmetry the lesson implied (top-level has Bash, sub-agent doesn't) does not exist. The two tiers (top-level, sub-agent) differ by exactly one tool: top-level has `Agent`, sub-agent doesn't. Both have the same ~10-name immediate tier (`Edit, Glob, Grep, Read, Skill, ToolSearch, Write` + visualize MCPs) and the same broad deferred tier (WebSearch, AskUserQuestion, all mcp__cowork__*, all mcp__workspace__*, all connector tools, etc.). New subsections added: (a) "Cowork-wide tool architecture" replacing the previous sub-agent-centric "Two-layer gate"; (b) "`mcp__workspace__bash` — the operational contract" documenting five operational constraints skill authors hit when moving CCD skills into Cowork — no cwd/env carryover between calls (each invocation independent), skill-path translation between host paths and `/sessions/<id>/mnt/` mounts (relative-path script invocations need `cd` chained or absolute mount paths), `outputs/` mount as the only persistence boundary surviving session end, `--break-system-packages` for pip, native-Mac-driving skills (Adobe etc.) using separate MCP servers not workspace bash; (c) Layer 2 (CLI async filter) reframed as documenting the real but Bash-irrelevant constraints — drop set $zH/r3H removes AskUserQuestion/ExitPlanMode/EnterPlanMode/Agent/TaskOutput/WaitForMcpServers from async sub-agents (top session keeps them), so it IS what makes sub-agents lose ability to ask user / dispatch nested sub-agents — but doesn't touch Bash. Implications section rewritten: declaring `tools: ["Bash"]` is a no-op in any Cowork dispatch level; canonical shell path is `mcp__workspace__bash` via ToolSearch; `Write`/`Edit` are portable artifact-persistence path; "move to top session" pattern doesn't give built-in Bash (top session also lacks it) but still has merit for absorbing intermediate work into parent context. Risks Worth Flagging items 6-7 split into 6 (no-built-in-Bash framing), 7 (operational contract), 8 (deferred-tier discovery via ToolSearch). Original v2.11.3 source-trace preserved as archaeology section. Companion gist (303b6213b7a33167b3f98b076a5f81ad) re-edited with same scope correction. v2.11.13: corrects L89 sub-agent-tool-grant trace — empirical claim was right (Bash unavailable in Cowork sub-agents), mechanism explanation was wrong. The original v2.11.3 trace claimed the `Tw8`/`Jl_` async filter strips Bash; binary re-extraction in v2.1.119 and v2.1.138 shows Bash IS in `Jl_`/`Ys_` via the spread member `VW = [wq, D9]` / `$2 = [Vq, h9]`. The actual gate is `HOST_LOOP_EXCLUDED_BUILTIN_TOOLS = jie = ["Bash", "NotebookEdit", "REPL", "JavaScript", "WebFetch"]` in the desktop bundle (`Claude.app/Contents/Resources/app.asar`), filtered by `PTi(tools) = tools.filter(t => t.startsWith("mcp__") || zvt.includes(t))` — Bash isn't in `zvt = HOST_LOOP_SAFE_BUILTIN_TOOLS`, so it's stripped at desktop-side tool registration before the SDK ever sees it. The desktop's workspace MCP server registers `mcp__workspace__bash` (literal: `psi = mcp__${WB="workspace"}__${Qy="bash"}`) and `mcp__workspace__web_fetch` as replacements. **New finding the original probe missed:** `mcp__workspace__bash` is in the deferred-tool tier in Cowork sub-agents — name visible, schema loaded on demand via `ToolSearch`. Skills that need shell from a fork-dispatch sub-agent in Cowork have a working path: declare `mcp__workspace__bash` in the agent's `tools:` array (literal exact match, no wildcards), call `ToolSearch` to load the schema, then invoke. v2.11.3–v2.11.12 of this lesson recommended only Write/Edit + move-shell-to-top-session; the MCP path is now also documented. Re-verified empirically: (a) host CLI v2.1.138 in CCD mode shows Bash IS reachable in async sub-agents (parent set to CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_KIND=bg, sub-agent dispatched via Task tool, response carries SendMessage continuation token confirming async, Bash visible and `echo PROBE_MARKER_<uuid>` succeeds) — proving the gate is desktop-side Cowork-specific, not CLI-side; (b) actual Cowork sub-agent reports `mcp__workspace__bash` as deferred tool — confirming the substitution. Two-layer model now documented in L89: Layer 1 (host-loop substitution, desktop bundle, Cowork-only) strips Bash + 4 other built-ins; Layer 2 (async sub-agent allowlist, CLI bundle, both modes) is downstream and irrelevant for Bash. v2.11.12: adds Cowork+scope rejection caveat to L26 per Codex round. The CLI rejects `--cowork` combined with any non-`user` scope (verified in v2.1.121 and v2.1.126: `--cowork can only be used with user scope` aborts the command). Manual `claude plugin update <id> --scope project --cowork` is NOT a valid workaround for project/local/managed Cowork installs — those are difficult to keep up to date through any standard path. Same caveat added to gist plus three more gist tightens: (a) gist's "Practical consequence" paragraph reworded to clarify Settings → Refresh / Update / Install do NOT fire refreshPluginMcps (only enable/disable, uninstall/delete, and local-upload variants do); (b) Short-version playbook recipes reworded to drop the misleading "toggle off/on fixes old content" implication — toggle off/on fires refreshPluginMcps but only reconciles org-plugin MCP connections, NOT skills/commands/agents/hooks; (c) Anthropic-managed-skills caveat added to the "new task" guidance (even a fresh task can stay stale until skills-plugin cache is repaired). v2.11.11: tightens L26 `refreshWarning` precision per Codex round on v1.5354.0+v2.1.121+v2.1.126. Previous wording said the CLI "captures this internally as a `refreshWarning` field on its update result." More precise: the warning text is captured in a local variable and concatenated into the update-result message string, not exposed as a structured field. Desktop's stdout parser doesn't extract it. Practical conclusion unchanged (Desktop hides marketplace refresh failures during update). Same correction also applied to public gist plus two additional gist fixes: (a) two passages still implied Settings → Refresh / Update fire refreshPluginMcps — corrected to "only enable/disable, uninstall/delete, and local-upload variants do; Refresh and Update do NOT"; (b) duplicate H1 lines re-stripped from gist top (regression from gh gist edit). v2.11.10: corrects refreshPluginMcps call-site enumeration in L26. v2.11.8 said it's invoked from "every state-mutating plugin op (~10 call sites: install / update / uninstall / setPluginEnabled / deletePlugin / etc.)". Bundle re-trace shows that's wrong: refreshPluginMcps is invoked from installPluginFromZip (local-upload), deletePlugin, setPluginEnabled, setRemotePluginEnabled, uninstallPlugin (both RPM and non-git fallback), and installLocalOrgPlugin — but NOT from the main installPlugin IPC handler or from updatePlugin. Settings → Install and Settings → Update do not fire the org-plugin MCP refresh. The corrected enumeration matters for downstream tooling: anyone relying on "click Update to refresh MCP" in a debugging flow would be surprised. Caught by Codex external review of v1.5354.0 + v2.1.126; bundle-verified by enumerating all 7 refreshPluginMcps() call sites under their containing dispatcher ops. v2.11.9: methodology correction + two doc fixes from Codex round on v2.1.121/v2.1.126 cross-check. (1) **Cowork uses Desktop-pinned VM-side binary, not standalone CLI on PATH.** Desktop pins its own Claude Code at `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude-code-vm/<sdk-version>/claude` (recorded in `.sdk-version`). At audit time Desktop is pinned to v2.1.121 while standalone CLI on PATH is v2.1.126. Cowork-internal traces (in-VM plugin commands, marketplace refresh, _syncSkills) should default to the pinned binary; standalone-CLI traces use PATH; Desktop main-process traces use app.asar. Behaviors in this lesson cross-check against both v2.1.121 and v2.1.126 — they haven't materially diverged for plugin management between those patch versions — but the methodology was wrong in earlier rounds (implicitly conflating the two binaries). Added explicit Cowork-binary callout to L26's Desktop-side cross-check intro and a methodology footnote at the top of the internal trace doc. (2) Corrected L26's `refreshWarning` claim — the CLI captures it internally but Desktop's `updatePlugin` IPC wrapper does NOT propagate it as a structured field. Wrapper returns only `{ success, pluginId, oldVersion, newVersion, alreadyUpToDate }`. Refresh warning is visible only in CLI log output. v2.11.8: extends L26 Desktop subsection with six findings accumulated since v2.11.7 across multiple Codex review rounds. (1) CLI's `claude plugin update` calls marketplace refresh with `skipIfRecent: true` (30-second window) and silently catches refresh failures, proceeding against cached clone with warn log "using cached data" — so update reports can be against stale data. (2) Desktop `updatePlugin` IPC has two paths: RPM-managed plugins go through remote-API `Hrt(r, marketplaceScope)`; non-RPM plugins fall through to `A(s,"git").updatePlugin(r, n)` which is the classic CLI shell-out without `--scope`. (3) Per-source-type badge path correction: string sources read at `<marketplace-clone>/<plugin.source>/`, NOT `<plugin-name>/` — `t_t` does `path.join(clone, entry.source)` for string. Object sources fall through to fallback path which usually misses then falls back to marketplace.json plugin entry. (4) `installed_plugins.json` v1→v2 migration is CLI-only; Desktop's native reader `V_(e)` returns parsed JSON as-is. Downstream code accesses `plugins[id][0]` which is `undefined` on v1 single-object values — plugins silently invisible in Desktop UI until CLI migrates the file. (5) Per-session `known_marketplaces.json` files exist at `local-agent-mode-sessions/<acc>/<org>/local_<UUID>/.claude/plugins/` written by the in-VM CLI (giveaway: VM-relative `installLocation` paths). Desktop IPC handlers do not consult these files. (6) HIGH-IMPACT: Anthropic-managed `skills-plugin/` cache at `local-agent-mode-sessions/skills-plugin/<orgId>/<accountId>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (note org/account order, opposite of Cowork plugin roots). Holds Cowork's built-in skills (pdf, xlsx, theme-factory, etc.). Sync timer 10 minutes. `_syncSkills` flow has silent-stale failure mode: per-skill download exception is caught and logged; `writeManifest` then runs unconditionally with new `updatedAt` for the failed skill; next sync sees matching `updatedAt` + existing-on-disk SKILL.md and skips redownload. Stale skill text persists indefinitely; Desktop restart doesn't fix it. Recovery: `rm` the stale SKILL.md, trigger sync. This cache is invisible to all other staleness checks (installed_plugins, marketplace.json, RPM, refreshPluginMcps). v2.11.7: corrects six errors in v2.11.6 surfaced by external bundle review (Codex). (1) Desktop badge does NOT read plugin.json from the install snapshot — `i_t` is called with 3 args (no options), so `t_t` skips its object-source branch (which requires the options arg) and returns the fallback path `<marketplace-clone>/<plugin-name>/`; for object-source plugins that file doesn't exist, so `i_t` falls back to `fte` returning the marketplace.json plugin entry. The badge is therefore keyed on `marketplace.json#plugins[].version` for object-source plugins, NOT on `plugin.json#version`. Bumping both fields is the reliable release pattern. (2) `refreshPluginMcps` is org-plugin MCP-only — `doRefreshPluginMcps` filters `source === "org-plugin"`. Settings UI ops do NOT trigger skill/command/agent/hook re-scan in running tasks; new task is the reliable boundary. (3) Desktop `updatePlugin` shells out without `--scope`, even though CLI accepts `-s, --scope <user|project|local|managed>` (default user) since v2.1.120. (4) installed_plugins schema v2 includes `managed` scope, `resolvedVersion` (tag-derived semver from version-constraint installs, used by verifyAndDemote), and `auto` boolean (true when pulled in as transitive dependency, eligible for orphan sweep). (5) Plugin-entry `npm` source supports `package` (broader than name — accepts URL/local path), optional `version`, optional `registry`. No `directory` plugin-entry variant. New `unsupported` placeholder for forward-compatible source rewrites. (6) Desktop parses CLI stdout for `"already up to date"` but CLI emits `"already at the latest version"` — `alreadyUpToDate: false` is wrongly returned to renderer on no-op success. Pre-existing string mismatch in both v2.1.120 and v2.1.126. v2.11.6: extends L26 with a Desktop-side cross-check (Claude.app v1.5354.0, main process at `.vite/build/index.js`). Desktop's `updatePlugin` IPC handler is a thin wrapper that shells out to `claude plugin update <id>` and parses stdout for "already up to date" and "from X to Y" — same K6H resolver end-to-end. Desktop's "Update available" badge in `listAvailablePlugins` uses K6H-equivalent priority (plugin.json#version primary, marketplace.json#plugins[].version fallback) via helpers `i_t` (read manifest), `t_t` (resolve plugin source dir), and `hte` (first-entry installPath lookup), but the plugin.json read location depends on source type: string sources read from `<marketplace-clone>/<source>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (live, badge surfaces after refresh); object sources (`github`/`url`/`git-subdir`/`directory` — the majority of public marketplaces) read from the install snapshot's path via `installed_plugins.json[id][0].installPath`, which is frozen at install time, so `pluginJson.version === installedVersion`, `availableVersion` is undefined, and the badge does not fire from this code path. CLI's `claude plugin update` still detects bumps because it operates on a freshly-fetched marketplace-clone view, not the install snapshot. Also corrects two prior misstatements: (a) the v2.11.5 framing of "Desktop UI vs CLI: two different version resolvers" was wrong — they have the same priority chain, just different read sources for object-source plugins; (b) the claude-plugin-doctor agent's interpretation of `agent/local_ditto_<uuid>/` as per-subagent state was wrong. Bundle: `Bm = "local_"`, `juA(e, A) = ${Bm}ditto_${e}` plus `_g${A}` for `A > 0`. The `_g<N>` suffix is bridge-rotation generation history at the **org level** (incremented on transport recovery, `resetModel`, clean-state restart); generations accumulate as historical state, only the latest is in active use. `local_<uuid>/` itself is per-session — `startSession` generates `local_<crypto-randomUUID>` when no sessionId is supplied. New internal note: docs/internal/desktop-bundle-trace-v1.5354.0.md. v2.11.5: documents `claude plugin update` version resolution (K6H, bundle offset 4,388,116). Five-level priority: (1) `<marketplace-clone>/<plugin-source>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json#version` PRIMARY, read fresh from disk via `B4_().manifest`; (2) `marketplace.json#plugins[].version` as `providedVersion` fallback (only fires when plugin.json missing or no version field); (3) pre-resolved git SHA (12-char; git-subdir variant adds 8-char path-hash); (4) computed git SHA via `ah1(path)` → `git rev-parse HEAD`; (5) `"unknown"` sentinel. Both installed snapshot (from `installed_plugins.json`) and fresh candidate go through K6H — no drift across the comparison. Comparison shape: `if (O.version === R || O.installPath === v || O.installPath === N)`. Corrects a common misreading (the inverted priority where marketplace.json#plugins[].version is treated as primary): under that read most github-source marketplaces would be no-op for `claude plugin update`, which contradicts observation. v2.11.4: extends the L90 sub-agent-tool-grant section with the MCP path. Documents the `yJ` MCP fast-path in `Tw8` (offset 5,036,218: `function yJ(H){return H.name?.startsWith('mcp__') || H.isMcp === true}` — first branch in the filter, MCP tools bypass `Jl_`/`F_8`/`r3H` unconditionally regardless of `isAsync`/`isBuiltIn`/`permissionMode`). Documents the parent→fork MCP-state inheritance flow: dispatch site at offset ~8,001,500 sets `availableTools = Ja(perm, w.getAppState().mcp.tools.concat(w.options.tools.filter(yJ)), {skipReplFilter: true})` for non-`/fork` paths; `Ja` (offset 8,711,381) returns `tR(perm, opts) + r8H(mcpTools, perm)` deduped by name — so Cowork-async forks inherit the parent's live MCP connections by reference, not by re-resolving .mcp.json. Documents the `requiredMcpServers` 30-second poll against `state.mcp.clients` (500ms interval, throws on `failed`/missing) — the runtime contract for the agent-frontmatter field. Negative finding: there is no skill-callable runtime MCP registration. `claude mcp add --scope dynamic` rejected at offset 7,368,245; `PW3()` chokidar setup at offset ~12,533,956 watches only skill/command directories (no `.mcp.json` watcher); `/mcp` exposes only reconnect/toggle on already-known servers; `--mcp-config` and SDK callbacks are out-of-band for skill bodies. Working pattern: static MCP declaration with a behaviorally dynamic launcher (server reads runtime state from env / `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` / stdin to vary tool listing across turns; the connection itself is fixed at session boot). Documents agent `tools:` exact-match constraint: `Sz`/`n0` chain does literal `f.get(name)` lookup, so `tools: ['mcp__server__*']` falls into `invalidTools` silently (the prefix form works in permission rules — validator at offset ~1,111,367 — but not in agent declarations). Five failure modes enumerated. v2.11.3: adds new L89 section 'Sub-Agent Tool-Grant Filtering: How Cowork-Async Dispatch Silently Strips Bash' — full source trace of the Tw8 base-tool filter, Jl_ allowlist (Bash absent), gN9/V9 fallback, F_8/r3H non-builtin drop set, vc() classifier with validTools/invalidTools/unavailableTools/resolvedTools buckets, Sz/n0 parser/canonicalizer pair, ev6 legacy-name rename map (Task→Agent), and Agent special-case K=false branch. Empirically confirmed by probe (write-a-file contract): plugin fork-skill with declared tools:['Read','Bash','Task','Glob','Grep'] fails persistence; same skill with Write/Edit added succeeds; general-purpose tools:['*'] inherits full filtered set and uses Write not Bash. Includes scope-clarification distinguishing the runtime tool filter from the body-time shell-substitution kill switch (CLAUDE_CODE_IS_COWORK policy logic). Also fixes preexisting topic-index.json gap: L89 and L90 had no index entries (claimed total_lessons:88 while ch20 had both); 275 new keywords across the two entries, semantic-index.json rebuilt to 1363 vocab terms. Companion gist updated through four rounds of external fact-check; no remaining hard factual errors per latest pass.
